sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Real Sociedad, currently sitting at 12th in the league with 43 points, host Celta Vigo, who are in 7th place with 49 points, at the Reale Arena in San Sebastian. This matchup promises to be a competitive encounter as both teams look to improve their standings. The referee for this game, Victor Garcia Verdura, is known for averaging 4.4 yellow cards and 0.19 red cards per game, which could influence the physicality of the clash. With Real Sociedad struggling recently, having not won in their last 5 matches, and Celta Vigo showing a tendency for high-scoring games with over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 matches, fans can expect an intriguing battle.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these two sides, Celta Vigo has secured 2 home wins, there have been 2 draws, and Real Sociedad managed 1 away win. Their most recent encounter on December 21, 2024, saw Celta Vigo triumph 2-0 at home, with Pablo Durán earning Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 7.9. Historically, matches between these teams often see under 2.5 goals, with this trend occurring in 4 of their last 5 meetings. Additionally, Real Sociedad has been the first to score in 7 of their last 8 head-to-head games, often taking control in the first half as winners in 4 of the last 5 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Real Sociedad are in a worrying slump, having failed to win in their last 5 matches and not scoring in their last 3 games, which could dent their confidence at home. They’ve also been the first to concede in 4 of their last 5 matches, signaling defensive vulnerabilities. On the other hand, Celta Vigo have been involved in high-scoring affairs, with over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 6 games, and both teams scoring in 5 of their last 7 matches. However, Celta Vigo’s discipline is notable, as they’ve had under 4.5 cards in all of their last 7 games, a trend mirrored by Real Sociedad in 5 of their last 7 matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Real Sociedad
sections.text: Real Sociedad will rely heavily on Mikel Oyarzabal, their forward with 8 goals scored this season, matching his expected goals of 8.00, showing clinical finishing. Sergio Gómez, a midfielder with a league rank of 5 for big chances created (14), is another player to watch with 5 assists and a high Sofascore rating of 7.12. Defender Jon Martin stands out with an impressive 82.5% aerial duels won, ranking 1st in the league, which could be crucial against Celta Vigo’s attacking threats. Additionally, Takefusa Kubo, with 5 goals and an average rating of 7.07, will look to exploit spaces in midfield, while Aritz Elustondo, a standout in the last 5 matchups against Celta with an average rating of 7.14, remains a defensive pillar.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Celta Vigo
sections.text: Celta Vigo’s attack is spearheaded by Borja Iglesias, who has netted 10 goals this season against an expected goals tally of 8.26, and is in fine form with 5 goals in his last 5 matches, boasting an average Sofascore rating of 7.675. Veteran forward Iago Aspas, with 9 goals and 4 assists, remains a constant threat with an average rating of 7.21, making him a key creative force. Óscar Mingueza, a midfielder with 6 assists and 4 goals, will aim to unlock Real Sociedad’s defense, while Williot Swedberg adds dynamism with 4 goals and 4 assists. However, the absence of Pablo Durán, who scored 4 goals this season, could impact their attacking depth.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Statistics
sections.text: Real Sociedad boast an impressive average ball possession of 54.29%, ranking 4th in the league, and excel in creating big chances with 64 this season, though their conversion rate of 31.25% ranks poorly at 18th. They’ve also recorded 13 clean sheets, ranking 2nd in the league, indicating defensive solidity at times. Celta Vigo, meanwhile, have shown a knack for late goals, with 18.52% of their goals (15 total) coming between the 76th and 90th minutes, suggesting they could push hard in the closing stages. Real Sociedad’s goals are more spread out, with 24.39% (11 goals) scored between the 46th and 60th minutes, so an early second-half push could be expected.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players and Impact
sections.text: Real Sociedad will miss forward Orri Steinn Óskarsson, who has 3 goals this season, and defender Nayef Aguerd, who ranks 2nd in the league for ground duels won at 72%, which could weaken their backline. Sheraldo Becker, another forward with 2 goals, is also sidelined, potentially limiting their attacking options. For Celta Vigo, the absence of Pablo Durán, with 4 goals and 4 assists, is a significant blow, as is the unavailability of experienced defender Marcos Alonso, who has a rating of 7.07. These absences could tilt the balance, especially in key defensive and attacking areas.

sections.subtitle: Discipline and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: Discipline could play a major role in this match, with referee Victor Garcia Verdura’s tendency to average 4.4 yellow cards per game suggesting a potentially card-heavy encounter. Real Sociedad have accumulated 81 yellow cards this season, ranking 12th for least yellows, while their average of 2.31 cards per game could rise given head-to-head trends of over 4.5 cards in 5 of the last 7 meetings between these teams. On the set-piece front, Real Sociedad average 5.4 corners per game, ranking 3rd in the league, but head-to-head stats show under 10.5 corners in 5 of their last 7 matchups, a trend also seen in 9 of Celta Vigo’s last 10 games.
